seed_problem: Build a connected relay network where no node has degree > 3, minimizing total cable length; checker validates connectivity+degree and scores baseline/cost.
mutation: output: keep the minimum-length degree-capped connectivity goal but strengthen the required output from a connected spanning network to a SURVIVABLE one -- a 2-edge-connected (bridgeless) spanning subgraph with per-module port caps in {2,3,4}. This turns the polynomial degree-bounded MST relaxation into the NP-hard minimum-length degree-constrained 2-edge-connected spanning subgraph problem.
title: Lunar Habitat Pressure Loops: Survivable Degree-Capped Tunnel Grid
strategies: loop baseline: connect the modules in input order into a single Hamiltonian cycle (always 2-edge-connected, degree 2 within caps, matches the checker baseline, ratio ~0.1)
nearest-neighbour tour: build a short Hamiltonian cycle by repeatedly hopping to the closest unvisited module, then close the loop
2-opt local search on the survivable tour: start from a nearest-neighbour cycle and apply length-reducing segment reversals that keep it a single cycle (still 2-edge-connected, degree 2)
port-spending ear/cross-link construction: exploit modules with cap 3-4 to add short chords/ears that shorten the loop while preserving bridgelessness
